  • SEARCH: 
 

Package i5.cb.api

  • Interface Summary 
    Interface Description
    ICBclient
    This interface allows CBclient objects to be used via RMI, in order to establish connections to a ConceptBase server through firewalls.
    IRemoteCBclientFactory
    interface for an RMI server which is able to dynamically create RemoteCBclient objects.
  • Class Summary 
    Class Description
    CBanswer
    A class to represent IPC answers of the ConceptBase server.
    CBclient
    represents an optionally RMI-able connection to a ConceptBase server.
    CBConnection
    represents an RMI-able connection to a ConceptBase server
    CBConnectionData
    connection data, as a shortcut.
    CBNotificationConnection
    A connection to a ConceptBase server via which the server sends notification messages to the client.
    CBterm  
    CButil
    A static class with some utility functions for ConceptBase.
    LocalCBclient
    A client class for ConceptBase.
    RemoteCBclient
    RMI server implementation for ICBclient.
    RemoteCBclientFactory
    RMI server implementation of IRemoteCBclientFactory, which is able to dynamically create RemoteCBclient objects.
  • Exception Summary 
    Exception Description
    CBConnectionBrokenException
    Thrown to indicate that the connection to the ConceptBase Server has been interrupted.
    CBConnectionException
    Thrown to indicate that the Connection to the ConceptBase Server could not be established for example because of DNS-Lookup Failure Applications can subclass this class to indicate similar exceptions
    CBDecodeException
    Thrown to indicate that the received values could not be decoded.
    CBEncodeException
    Thrown to indicate that the received values could not be encoded Applications can subclass this class to indicate similar exceptions
    CBIOException
    Thrown to indicate that an IO Exception appeared.
    CBMessageException
    Thrown to indicate that the requested message could not be handled by the ConceptBase Server or the incoming message could not be handled by the CBclient.
    CBtermParserException
    Thrown to indicate that the term could not be parsed.
    CBTimeOutException
    Thrown to indicate that the Connection to the ConceptBase Server timed out or could not handle request in a forced amount of time.
    CBUtilException
    Thrown to indicate that an util related Exception appeared.
